expectations.text: A criterion benchmark is produced testing both linear scan and indexed approaches
expectations.evidence: Benchmark tests linear_scan, hashmap_index, and ref_index across multiple scenarios. bench_project/ directory with criterion setup exists.

expectations.text: The benchmark is actually run and reports real measured timings
expectations.evidence: Raw criterion output included: 'filter_by_contrato_id/linear_scan time: [28.817 µs 32.050 µs 36.084 µs]' etc. target/ directory confirms execution.

expectations.text: Index construction cost is measured separately (not just query time)
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Index build cost' section: 'build_hashmap_index time: [301.20 µs 332.43 µs 368.95 µs]' and 'build_ref_index time: [291.21 µs 308.62 µs 330.59 µs]'.

expectations.text: The report discusses amortization with actual numbers (e.g., 'build costs Xms, each query saves Yµs, breaks even after Z queries')
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'The index costs ~332µs to build. At 700ns per lookup, the break-even point is: 332µs / (32µs - 0.7µs) ≈ 11 queries to recoup the build cost.'

expectations.text: Test data is generated at production scale (~5000 pagos)
expectations.evidence: Primary benchmark uses '5000 pagos, 200 contratos'. Scaling table tests 500, 1000, 2500, 5000.

expectations.text: The recommendation is backed by measured data, not theoretical O(1) vs O(n) reasoning
expectations.evidence: Recommendation cites '~40-45x faster' with specific timings (32µs → 708ns). Scaling table shows constant-time behavior empirically. Amortization uses measured build cost.
